description: Test 8 hand-curated historical bridges (Zorn/Choice, Galois<->Covering, Stone duality, Pontryagin duality, fixed-point, Gromov-Hausdorff, Spectral<->Representation, Lie algebras<->Lie groups): do the two sides land in distinct clusters, and is any matching theorem flagged (strict = depth-filtered theta<=2; loose = any bridge output)?
motivation: External validation against historically celebrated cross-domain connections.
formal_results: 7/8 span distinct clusters (Zorn/Choice does not - both sides fall together); 6/8 loose-detected; only 2/8 survive strict theta<=2 (Gromov-Hausdorff, Spectral<->Representation). Galois<->Covering recovered as PreGaloisCategory.has_decomp_connected_components is the strongest qualitative case.
results_description: Most known bridges span distinct clusters and are loosely detected; few survive strict filtering.
conclusions: The method aligns with history: 7/8 span distinct clusters and 6/8 are loosely detected, validating operational-bridge detection - though only 2/8 pass the strict filter, showing the strict criterion is conservative.

comments.counts: Precise: 7/8 span distinct clusters, 6/8 loose-detected, 2/8 strict (theta<=2). The README's '6 of 8' figure is the loose-detection count.
